columns.label: canonical reference
columns.what: The library that defines the ground truth for each method (sklearn for `pls`/`pcr`, ropls for `opls`, mixOmics for `*_da`, …). The cell value mirrors the canonical library's timing for that row and is language-tinted accordingly.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.native
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_BLAS=OFF, OPENMP=OFF` — pure scalar native C++ loops, no acceleration. Used as one C++ implementation column; binding parity still uses cpp @ blas-omp when available.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.blas
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_BLAS=ON` only — links system BLAS (OpenBLAS), benefits from BLAS thread parallelism.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.omp
columns.what: libp4a built with `PLS4ALL_WITH_OPENMP=ON` only — OpenMP in C kernel loops, no BLAS.

columns.label: pls4all.cpp.blas+omp
columns.what: libp4a built with both `BLAS=ON` and `OPENMP=ON` — the recommended production config.

columns.label: pls4all.registry
columns.what: `benchmarks.parity_timing.registry.MethodSpec.pls4all_fn` — the canonical pls4all invocation defined by the benchmark suite for each algorithm (algorithm + solver + deflation + …). This is the column used as the parity reference against sklearn / pls / ropls / plsregress.

columns.label: pls4all.python
columns.what: `pls4all._methods.<algo>_fit(ctx, cfg, X, y, …)` — direct FFI binding

columns.label: pls4all.R
columns.what: `pls4all_method(algo, X, y, ...)` — unified dispatcher (33 fits + 24 selectors + 4 diagnostics)

columns.label: pls4all.R.pls_compat
columns.what: `plsr()` / `pcr()` for PLS/PCR/CPPLS, formula-built dispatcher path for the rest of the method matrix

columns.label: pls4all.R.mdatools_compat
columns.what: `pls(x, y, ...)` for PLS/PCR/CPPLS, matrix dispatcher path for the rest of the method matrix

fused_sparse_pls.paper_only: Yengo, L., Jacques, J., Biernacki, C. & Canouil, M. (2016). Variable clustering in high-dimensional linear regression: The R package `clere`. R Journal 8(1), 92-106. (Fused-sparse PLS variants have no widely installable R / Python port.)

mir_pls.paper_only: Sjöblom, J., Svensson, O., Josefson, M., Kullberg, H. & Wold, S. (1998). An evaluation of orthogonal signal correction applied to calibration transfer of near infrared spectra. Chemom. Intell. Lab. Syst. 44(1-2), 229-244. (Inverse-regression PLS variant; no widely installable port.)

scars_select.paper_only: Zheng, K., Zhang, X., Tong, P., Yao, Y. & Du, Y. (2014). Pretreating near infrared spectra with fractional order Savitzky-Golay differentiation (FOSGD). Chemom. Intell. Lab. Syst. 132, 30-38. (SCARS hybrid; no widely installable port — smoke-tested.)

sipls_select.paper_only: Nørgaard, L., Saudland, A., Wagner, J., Nielsen, J. P., Munck, L. & Engelsen, S. B. (2000). Interval partial least-squares regression (iPLS). Appl. Spectrosc. 54(3), 413-419. (No widely installable R / Python port of siPLS' synergistic combinations; smoke-tested only.)

binding_parity.name: binding-consistency gate
binding_parity.description: Element-wise max|pred-ref| <= tolerance. Asserts every pls4all binding produces bit-identical outputs to the C kernel.

reference_parity.name: external-reference validity gate
reference_parity.description: Relative-RMSE ||pred-ref||_RMS / ||ref||_RMS <= tolerance, with an absolute-RMSE <= 1e-9 escape near zero. Asserts pls4all's algorithm output matches an external ground-truth implementation within the method's declared tolerance band.

suites.description: Fastest cross-binding cells for every MethodSpec; used by pre-commit / CI smoke gates.
Full cross-binding sweep - every MethodSpec across every default dataset size. Mirrors the cross-binding orchestrator's `DEFAULT_SIZES` surface.
